build_from.reason: Epic 28.0 (completed) established the content-free telemetry contract as a hard-locked, exactly-4-field `Event` struct with no omitempty tags, guarded by a dedicated allowlist test. This epic's quality-signal payload (per-persona+model counters and identifiers) must follow the identical allowlist pattern — a new, equally strict struct — never extend or embed the existing Event or any scorecard type.
build_from.suggested_approach: Add a new allowlisted payload type (e.g. `telemetry.QualitySignal` or similar) in `internal/telemetry/` alongside `event.go`, aggregate `dismissed`/`confirmed` counts per persona+model from the Epic 24.0 debt/dismissal records (`internal/localdebt` — `wontfix`/`resolved` terminal status on the append-only JSONL records in the `.atcr/debt/` store; NOT `internal/registry`, which holds only config), gate transmission behind an explicit new opt-in (separate from the existing passive-ping `ATCR_TELEMETRY` gate and the existing `--sync-cloud` opt-in), and add a `--preview` surface that renders the exact outbound payload without sending it.

existing_patterns.name: Allowlisted telemetry payload
existing_patterns.description: `internal/telemetry/event.go`'s `Event` struct has exactly 4 fields, no `omitempty`, and a dedicated test (`TestClient_Send_PayloadHasExactlyFourAllowlistedKeys`) that fails if a field is added — the mechanism that makes "content-free" enforceable rather than just documented.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Any new outbound payload type this epic introduces for persona+model quality counters.

existing_patterns.name: Cloud-sync allowlist payload keyed by persona_id_hash + model (Epic 28.0)
existing_patterns.description: `internal/scorecard/cloudsync.go`'s `CloudSyncRecord` carries a `Personas []CloudSyncPersona` list whose entries ALREADY carry `persona_id_hash` + `model` (plus cost/token/latency metrics) — directly answering plan.md's open question. `Push` POSTs it with an `Authorization: Bearer` header, fail-open except auth rejection. `internal/scorecard/telemetry.go`'s `HashPersonaID` (unsalted SHA-256, documented dictionary-attack caveat TD-007) is the pseudonymization primitive. This is the transport and keying shape the quality signal rides.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Deciding (design-sprint) whether the new dismissed/confirmed counters EXTEND the existing `CloudSyncPersona`/`CloudSyncRecord` shape or ship as a sibling allowlisted payload over the same `Push` transport; reusing `HashPersonaID` for persona identifiers in either case.

existing_patterns.name: Independent opt-in surfaces, OR-combined, never silently overridden
existing_patterns.description: `cmd/atcr/telemetry.go`'s `telemetryEnabled`/`telemetryGate` implement a documented truth table combining an env var and a persisted config flag, and explicitly note that the passive-ping gate must NOT gate the separate `--sync-cloud` explicit-action opt-in — each transmission mechanism gets its own consent surface. `cmd/atcr/cloudsync.go`'s `resolveSyncCloud` is the second example: flag + `ATCR_API_KEY` presence, deliberately NOT gated by telemetryGate.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Wiring this epic's new opt-in flag/config key as a THIRD independent surface, without accidentally coupling it to the existing passive-ping or --sync-cloud gates.

existing_patterns.name: wontfix/resolved terminal status on localdebt records (Epic 24.0)
existing_patterns.description: `atcr debt resolve --status wontfix --reason ...` (cmd/atcr/debt_resolve.go) records a finding's dismissal by folding it into the same terminal-status lifecycle as `resolved`, with `wontfix` outranking `resolved` when both are present (`closedStatusRank`). The records live in the append-only JSONL store at `.atcr/debt/` whose schema is `internal/localdebt/record.go`. CONFIRMED attribution state: each record carries `Reviewers []string` (raw persona names — persona attribution PRESENT) but NO model field; model is recoverable only indirectly via the record's `RunID` → the run's pool summary (`fanout.ReadPoolSummary`, `internal/fanout/status.go` `AgentStatus.Model`).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Reading the raw per-finding dismissal signal to aggregate into per-persona+model counters. Design-sprint must decide whether model attribution is derived via RunID→pool-summary join or added as an explicit field on new localdebt records (schema_version bump).

existing_patterns.name: Per-(reviewer, model) aggregation idiom
existing_patterns.description: `internal/scorecard/aggregate.go`'s `Aggregate()` groups per-reviewer records by a `(reviewer, model)` key and sums them into leaderboard rows — the existing in-repo idiom for exactly the grouping dimension this epic needs.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Structuring the dismissed/confirmed counter aggregation per persona+model.

existing_patterns.name: Cobra subcommand + render pattern
existing_patterns.description: `cmd/atcr/report.go` (`newReportCmd`/`runReport`) is the existing pattern for a maintainer-facing rendering subcommand with format flags.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Structuring the new maintainer-facing report surface (AC2) and the `--preview` flag (AC1) as a thin Cobra subcommand, consistent with the dispatcher conventions.

related_files.purpose: Background, fail-open HTTP client that sends telemetry.Event payloads; one of the two candidate transports this epic rides per Epic 28.0.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-only, or extend cautiously if a new send path is needed for the aggregate signal

related_files.purpose: Defines the sole allowlisted telemetry payload today (4 fields: event, lang, lines, status).
related_files.likely_modification: extend with a new sibling allowlisted type, not by modifying Event itself

related_files.purpose: Epic 28.0 cloud-sync transport: `CloudSyncRecord`/`CloudSyncPersona` allowlist payload (personas entries already carry `persona_id_hash` + `model`), `NewCloudSyncRecord` builder, `Push` authenticated POST, `ValidateCloudEndpoint`. The transport the plan says this signal rides.
related_files.likely_modification: extend with dismissed/confirmed counters (if the extend-payload decision wins) or reference-only (if a sibling payload type is created)

related_files.purpose: `HashPersonaID` (one-way SHA-256 persona pseudonymization) and `TelemetryPersonaRecord` — the identifier-hashing primitive the quality signal must reuse so raw persona names never leave the machine.

CLI wiring for `--sync-cloud`: `resolveSyncCloud` (fail-fast precondition check), `runSyncCloud`, `syncCloudPlan`, and the exit-code mapping (`usageError`/`authError`) that keeps a push failure from corrupting the run outcome.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-pattern for wiring the new opt-in transmission into review/reconcile flows, or extend if the signal piggybacks on the sync-cloud push

related_files.purpose: Opt-in gating logic (telemetryEnabled/telemetryGate) and event-building helpers for the existing passive ping.
related_files.likely_modification: extend with a parallel, independent opt-in gate for the quality-signal transmission

related_files.purpose: Loads/persists the `.atcr/config.yaml` telemetry opt-out setting used by telemetryGate (LoadTelemetrySetting/SetTelemetrySetting, atomic config write under config.lock).
related_files.likely_modification: extend or add a sibling setting for the new opt-in

related_files.purpose: `atcr config set` surface — today its settable-key allowlist is exactly `telemetry`; persisting a new opt-in key (e.g. `quality_signal`) requires extending this allowlist.
related_files.likely_modification: extend the settable-key allowlist and help text for the new opt-in key

related_files.purpose: Schema of the `.atcr/debt/` append-only JSONL dismissal store: `Record` carries `Reviewers []string` (persona attribution) plus `Status`/`ResolvedAt` terminal fields, but NO model field. SchemaVersion=1 governs evolution.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-only for aggregation; extend schema (with version bump) only if the design-sprint chooses stored model attribution over RunID-derived attribution

related_files.purpose: Implements `atcr debt resolve --status wontfix|resolved` — the write path of the raw dismissed/confirmed events this epic aggregates per Epic 24.0.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-only unless persona/model attribution needs to be added to the debt record schema

related_files.purpose: `Aggregate()` groups scorecard records by (reviewer, model) — the existing per-persona+model grouping idiom.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-only pattern for the counter aggregation

related_files.purpose: `AgentStatus` (per-agent `Model` field) read via `fanout.ReadPoolSummary` (`internal/fanout/artifacts.go:180`) — the indirect model-attribution source for localdebt records, joined by `RunID`.
Existing maintainer-facing report rendering subcommand pattern.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-only pattern for the new report/preview surface

related_files.purpose: Documents the existing content-free telemetry contract (event schema, opt-out mechanism).
related_files.likely_modification: extend with the new quality-signal contract (fields, opt-in mechanism, preview behavior, no-code/no-finding-content line) per AC3

related_files.purpose: Community persona definitions (one *.yaml per persona under personas/community/, plus index.json; fields: name, model, provider — e.g. `anthony.yaml`: provider openrouter, model anthropic/claude-opus-4.8) — the keying dimension (persona+model) for the aggregated counters.

integration_points.description: Point where a finding's terminal dismissal/confirmation status is recorded; this epic's aggregation reads from here (directly or via a derived event) to build per-persona+model counters.

The actual persisted dismissal data: append-only records with `Reviewers []string` (persona) and terminal `Status`. Model attribution requires a RunID→pool-summary join (`fanout.ReadPoolSummary`) or a schema extension.
Existing fail-open HTTP send path (Epic 28.0) that this epic's opt-in aggregate payload can reuse rather than building a new transport.

The Epic 28.0 `--sync-cloud` authenticated push and its persona-keyed payload — the primary transport candidate the plan names. Design-sprint decides extend-existing-payload vs. sibling-payload over this same Push path.
Where an explicit, user-invoked transmission is validated and fired after run finalization — the wiring template if the quality signal sends on the review/reconcile path.
Pattern for a dedicated, independent opt-in gate — this epic needs its own gate function analogous to telemetryGate, not reuse of the passive-ping gate.
Per-agent `Model` values for a run; joined to localdebt records via `RunID` when model attribution is derived rather than stored.

integration_gaps.gap: Dismissal records carry no model attribution
integration_gaps.impact: The epic's keying dimension is persona+model, but the `.atcr/debt/` store records only `Reviewers []string`; per-persona+model counters cannot be computed from the store alone.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Design-sprint picks one: derive model via each record's `RunID` → `fanout.ReadPoolSummary` `AgentStatus.Model` join (no schema change), or add a model field to new localdebt records with a schema_version bump (only future dismissals attributable).

integration_gaps.gap: `atcr config set` allowlist admits only the `telemetry` key
integration_gaps.impact: The new independent opt-in cannot be persisted as a config setting without extending the settable-key allowlist and its help text.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Extend cmd/atcr/config.go to accept the new quality-signal opt-in key, mirroring the telemetry key's validation and SetTelemetrySetting-style persistence.

integration_gaps.gap: Payload shape for the counters is an unresolved fork
integration_gaps.impact: Plan.md defers to design-sprint whether dismissed/confirmed counters extend the existing CloudSyncPersona shape (persona_id_hash+model already present) or ship as a sibling allowlisted payload; files_to_create/modify cannot be finalized until then.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Resolve at design-sprint; either way the payload must be a fixed-field allowlisted struct with its own locking regression test, and persona identifiers must go through scorecard.HashPersonaID.

integration_gaps.gap: No existing preview/dry-run surface for outbound payloads
integration_gaps.impact: AC1's `--preview` requirement (render the exact outbound payload locally, send nothing) has no existing mechanism to reuse; neither the passive ping nor --sync-cloud has a dry-run today.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Add a --preview flag (addSyncCloudFlags-style registration) that json.Marshal's the exact payload struct and prints it, short-circuiting before any send.

semantic_matches.relevance: Existing (reviewer, model) grouping idiom — the exact accumulation shape the per-persona+model dismissed/confirmed counters need.

reusable_components.name: telemetry.Client
reusable_components.usage_example: Reuse the existing fail-open HTTP client for sending the new aggregate quality-signal payload once opted in.

reusable_components.name: registry telemetry setting persistence
reusable_components.usage_example: Mirror this file's load/persist pattern for a new, independent quality-signal opt-in setting in `.atcr/config.yaml`.

reusable_components.name: scorecard.HashPersonaID
reusable_components.usage_example: Reuse as-is to hash persona identifiers in the quality-signal payload so raw persona names never leave the machine; inherits the documented unsalted-SHA-256 dictionary caveat (TD-007).

reusable_components.name: scorecard (reviewer, model) grouping idiom
reusable_components.usage_example: Copy the map-keyed-by-(persona, model) accumulation pattern for the dismissed/confirmed counters.

architecture_notes: The privacy line ("zero code and zero finding content ever leaves the machine") is currently enforced structurally, not just documented: Event has exactly 4 allowlisted fields with a dedicated regression test. The new quality-signal type must adopt the same enforcement pattern (fixed field set, no omitempty, an allowlist test) so it is falsifiable, not just claimed.

Existing telemetry opt-in surfaces are deliberately independent and non-overriding (passive ping vs. --sync-cloud). This epic's opt-in must be a third, independent surface — do not fold it into telemetryGate or the --sync-cloud gate.

CONFIRMED (was an open question): Epic 24.0 dismissal records live in `internal/localdebt` (append-only JSONL at `.atcr/debt/`, schema_version 1), NOT in `internal/registry` and NOT in `internal/debt` (that is the separate, private `.planning/technical-debt/` pipeline). Persona attribution IS present (`Reviewers []string`); model attribution is NOT stored — it must be derived via `RunID` → `fanout.ReadPoolSummary` `AgentStatus.Model`, or added to the record schema with a version bump. Design-sprint must pick one.

CONFIRMED (was an open question): `--sync-cloud`'s `CloudSyncPersona` entries already carry `persona_id_hash` + `model`, so the dismissed/confirmed counters can either extend that existing shape or ship as a sibling allowlisted payload over the same `Push` transport — plan.md explicitly defers this choice to design-sprint.

Persona identifiers in the new payload should be hashed with the existing `scorecard.HashPersonaID` (unsalted SHA-256). That function carries a documented dictionary-attack caveat over the small enumerable persona-name set (TD-007, HMAC hardening deferred to endpoint activation); the quality signal inherits the same caveat and should be documented as such in docs/telemetry.md.

files_to_modify.reason: Add the new independent opt-in gate for the quality-signal transmission, following the telemetryEnabled/telemetryGate pattern.
Add persistence for the new quality-signal opt-in setting.
Extend the `atcr config set` settable-key allowlist (today exactly `telemetry`) and help text so the new opt-in is user-persistable.
Only if design-sprint chooses to extend the existing CloudSyncRecord/CloudSyncPersona payload with dismissed/confirmed counters (vs. a sibling payload type over the same Push transport).
Document the new content-free quality-signal contract per AC3.
Register the new --preview flag for the local preview surface (per /refine-epic's flagged missing file), following the addSyncCloudFlags pattern.

files_to_create.purpose: New allowlisted payload type for per-persona+model dismissed/confirmed aggregate counters, mirroring event.go's strict-schema pattern.
New maintainer-facing surface identifying over/under-reporting personas+models, plus the --preview rendering of exactly what would be sent (AC1, AC2).
